steps.title: Find the slope from two table points
Build the equation and clear the denominator
steps.content: Point–slope through \((s,13)\):$$y-13=-\frac{5}{s}(x-s)\Rightarrow y=-\frac{5}{s}x+18.$$Multiply both sides by \(s\) and rearrange:$$sy=-5x+18s\;\Rightarrow\;5x+sy=18s.$$Check with the third point \((-s,23)\): \(5(-s)+s(23)=-5s+23s=18s.\) \(\checkmark\)

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) \(sx+5y=18s\): plug in \((s,13)\) → \(s^2+65\), not \(18s\) for general \(s\) — fails.
  • B) \(5x+sy=18s\): all three table points satisfy it (verified above).
  • C) \(5x+sy=18\): plug \((s,13)\) → \(5s+13s=18s\ne 18\) unless \(s=1\) — fails.
  • D) \(sx+5y=18\): plug \((s,13)\) → \(s^2+65\ne 18\) — fails.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
